Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 268 upvotes, 24 comments
  2. Human Organ Atlas 68 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. LLM Architecture Gallery 32 upvotes, 1 comments
  4. In Memoriam: John W. Addison, my PhD advisor 45 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. Learning athletic humanoid tennis skills from imperfect human motion data 63 upvotes, 7 comments
  6. Let your Coding Agent debug the browser session with Chrome DevTools MCP 52 upvotes, 8 comments
  7. Rack-mount hydroponics 320 upvotes, 86 comments
  8. Zipp 2001 Restoration 22 upvotes, 2 comments
  9. Show HN: GDSL – 800 line kernel: Lisp subset in 500, C subset in 1300 39 upvotes, 7 comments
  10. Bus travel from Lima to Rio de Janeiro 30 upvotes, 5 comments
  11. Show HN: Signet – Autonomous wildfire tracking from satellite and weather data 94 upvotes, 27 comments
  12. Kniterate Notes 44 upvotes, 10 comments
  13. IBM, sonic delay lines, and the history of the 80×24 display (2019) 61 upvotes, 16 comments
  14. Generating All 32-Bit Primes (Part I) 64 upvotes, 20 comments
  15. A most elegant TCP hole punching algorithm 195 upvotes, 78 comments
  16. Why Mathematica does not simplify sinh(arccosh(x)) 130 upvotes, 49 comments
  17. Separating the Wayland compositor and window manager 119 upvotes, 48 comments
  18. C++26: The Oxford Variadic Comma 55 upvotes, 19 comments
  19. Allow me to get to know you, mistakes and all 284 upvotes, 140 comments
  20. Glassworm Is Back: A New Wave of Invisible Unicode Attacks Hits Repositories 143 upvotes, 80 comments
  21. Show HN: What if your synthesizer was powered by APL (or a dumb K clone)? 55 upvotes, 27 comments
  22. Grandparents are glued to their phones, families are worried [video] 115 upvotes, 72 comments
  23. UMD Scientists Create 'Smart Underwear' to Measure Human Flatulence 72 upvotes, 45 comments
  24. How kernel anti-cheats work 323 upvotes, 278 comments
  25. Autoresearch Hub 10 upvotes, 3 comments
  26. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 122 upvotes, 95 comments
  27. $96 3D-printed rocket that recalculates its mid-air trajectory using a $5 sensor 323 upvotes, 300 comments
  28. The 100 hour gap between a vibecoded prototype and a working product 192 upvotes, 251 comments
  29. Treasure hunter freed from jail after refusing to turn over shipwreck gold 156 upvotes, 216 comments
  30. Hollywood Enters Oscars Weekend in Existential Crisis 75 upvotes, 235 comments

Last generated 4:00am, 16 March 2026. Source here.
